channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 232 replies — 928.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 232 • Updated: 2026-04-02 13:41 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62048850
>>62048431
>You mean there are none?
What would you regard as "influential news"?

62048939
>>62048850
What do you think? Can you define influential

62048959
>>62048939
>Can you define influential
No, because it's a subjective word. To some people a towns hall meting of a town of 500 people might be the most "influential" thing in the world since it affect them, their home town and their lives. To someone else it's a completely irrelevant event because it doesn't affect anyone or anything in their own lives. To some people the war in Iran is completely unimportant and doesn't affect them. To some people, like the Iranians is a life changing event.

The word "influential" news is in the perception of the beholder. I can give you as much stuff as you want, it's just... If you don't care about it then it's not "influental", that's why I'm asking
>What would you regard as "influential news"?
Since every individual themselves need to determine what THEY think is influential for them and tickles their funny bone.

62049007
>>62048939
Here, a google definiton; Influential describes
someone or something with the power to cause change, shape decisions, or exert significant impact on people or events. It implies having high status, authority, or being highly important (e.g., an influential leader or book).

62049042
>>62048939
>>62045442
>>62048708
If you can't even define what you want then how can I even give it to you, right? I can only give you something you can actually define.

62049654
>>62048959
>>62049007
>>62049042

You literally shill this fucking coin. Do some influencing

62051513
>>62049654
Yes. Define "influencing". What would INFLUENCE YOU? Naked ladies? Hackatons? Drugs? New technical developments? What influences YOU? If I just post something then you'll be like "No, that's not good enough", so what's the actual point if I can't give you EXACTLY what you want?

62051763
>>62051513
From reading the thread I thought you had some intellectual insight, nope an idiot

62051818
>>62051763
>From reading the thread I thought you had some intellectual insight
You tell me what you think is an "intellectual insight"? What would convince you? Throwing a hissy fit and getting emotional at me doesn't really work...

62052015
>>62051763
I'm not sure if I'm making myself clear enough. Define what you think would constitute for something that YOU consider an "intellectual insight" in your own mind? Can you post a piece of information from another chain that amazed you, that made you think, that made you say "Oh shit, this is lit"? I hope I've made myself clear enough good sar.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056424
>>62051763
100$ end of bull run. Here, some influencing for you.


Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056570
>>62051763
>>62049654
>>62048939
>>62048708
>>62045442
Here you go. Let me influence you young Vishnus of the mindchain; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU
DOT will become the Web3 cloud. It'll literally be able to do work for ETH, which means it'll be able to scale ETH to millions of TPS. ETH will be able to forward transactions to DOT and DOT will be able to execute them for ETH therefore scaling ETH to millions of TPS. DOT will be able to do that for any arbitrary project (not sure about stuff like BTC and DOGE that has no smart contracts. Maybe that too, yet I can't comment on that because I really don't know at this point).

62056612
>>62056570
>DOT will become the Web3 cloud. It'll literally be able to do work for ETH, which means it'll be able to scale ETH to millions of TPS. ETH will be able to forward transactions to DOT and DOT will be able to execute them for ETH therefore scaling ETH to millions of TPS. DOT will be able to do that for any arbitrary project (not sure about stuff like BTC and DOGE that has no smart contracts. Maybe that too, yet I can't comment on that because I really don't know at this point).
This is JUST one of the possible applications. Of course you'll be able to execute ANY arbitrary operation on-chain, like any Web2 cloud can. Imagine a WHOLE GAME deployed on-chain. Literally every step of your character, every emote, every chat message, every loot drop and pick up being on-chain. You'll of course also be able to run AI agents and shit like that... DOT is the ONLY chain capable of doing MILLIONS of TPS once NOMT + JAM get implemented
NOMT alone will x10 the throughput of the whole DOT chain. https://www.rob.tech/blog/introducing-nomt/ and https://github.com/thrumdev/nomt and https://www.reddit.com/r/Polkadot/comments/1nzjhuq/what_has_happened_to_nomt/ and https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N3BX2eTqpro

So what's getting shipped this year that's considered "influential" for out young Vishnus of the mindchain? JAM, NOMT, the Polkadot App and more stuff (instant unstaking (unstaking will go from 28 days do 1-2 days)), project individuality (proof of personhood that doesn't require KYC, 500ms block (Basti blocks), and more)

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62056623
How do I buy this shit and how long do I hold

62056631
When will you incels realize shitcoins only go up if bitcoin goes up, it doesn’t matter how much ‘bullish’ news a shitcoin gets

62056642
>>62056623
What do you mean "How do I buy this shit"? Bro, if this comes of as mean, it's not meant as mean and I'm sorry if it sounds mean. If you can't figure out how to buy something yourself then please just don't buy it at all. Make yourself a favor

>>62056631
You do realize that this won't be true forever? And that certain projects do decouple from BTC when they're ripe for it.

62056686
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gg4P23PirgU

Oliver makes a really good point here where he tries to point out that like 4 years ago L2s could just exist and people would use them and NOW people are demanding that the L2s upgrade and improve in order to still be considered useful. At this point I would like to add an additional thing. The thing that people want from major L2s (like Arbitrum and AVAX and other stuff like that) is what DOT L2s already have. The major L2s can't compete with DOT because they're behind and once JAM hits they'll be even more behind. In the time DOT gets JAM the L2s won't even have what DOT has RIGHT NOW. DOT has shared security, it has built-in native SAFE bridging (that's not even a bridge, it's a XCM, it uses HRMP channels), because of the shared security it DOES NOT fragment liquidity, you can use the functionality of ANOTHER chain on DOT (so of other DOT chains and other chains in general that aren't on DOT). Once people realize that they can EASILY build on DOT while every other chains LIMITS them in some shape or form they'll start flocking to DOT. Every other chain has a ton of problems that DOT simply DOES NOT therefore DOT is technologically speaking superior. Sooner or later the developers will catch onto it and start developing on DOT since the other chains have limited capabilities. You can only develop humdrum applications on ETH, no innovation will ever come out of ETH ever again. ETH is obselete and unreconcilable. Sure it'll stay relevant for a few more year but how much longer can it stand the test of time? BTC is windows 1.0, ETH is windows 98, DOT is Windows 12 lol (Yes, not Windows 11, it's windows 12).

62056998
>>62056631
The game is changing. This bear market will prove it

62057110
>>62056998
>The game is changing.
What do you see anon? Help us understand what you see when you say "The game is changing". And yes, I do agree that stuff will shift from stupidity back to usability. So, meme coins were the LAST DROP. Enough is ENOUGH. We need to go back to our roots and invest into stuff that actually makes sense and is GROUND BREAKING. If we don't pivot in this liminal time then the industry as a whole will be finished for like 10-20 years UNTIL we pivot there is no way forward. We need to REALIZE that BTC is crap, ETH is crap, SOL is crap, USDT is centralized shit, XRP is useless, TRX is a cult run piece of shit, lol BCH is in the top 10 WTF, DOGE is crap, BNB is fucking crap. It's all just useless junk. Literally the whole of top 10 is fucking garbage that's unusable. It's literally all Windows 1 and Windows 98 type of coins while we already have the Vishnu of the mindchain, the Windows 12 of blockchains, the collectibe 1/9 Lamborghini Veneno Roadster of the blockchain, the fucking Rolls-Royce of the blockchain industry, and we're still stuck with the fucking Windows 1 and Windows 98 of the blockchain industry. Guys, it's time for FUCKING CHANGE. Either we pivot to the 3rd generation blockchains or the whole sector will GO UNDER for a decade or two. Do you want to suck cock for 10 to 20 years? Or do you just market sell your garbage gen 1 and gen 2 tokens/coins and move on with your life and buy the actual technical innovation? It's choice time and it's GOOD to know that any day you can change your whole fucking life. If you want the world to change then the first step to changing the world is to CHANGE YOUR FUCKING SELF. You only need to 1. WANT THE CHANGE and 2. BELIEVE YOU CAN CHANGE (Self-efficacy). Those are the 2 most important principles of BEHAVIOURAL CHANGE. Change your behaviour and with that you'll change the world. Sure it'll take time and effort but hey, even wallowing in your misery, being depressed takes time and effort.

62058962
>>62056631
Algorand is pumping while Bitcoin is dumping because it is quantum secure. Nigger

62059225
DOT will reach the $1.60 range before this week ends

62059268
>>62057110
See was that so hard on being influential?

62060540
>>62059268
The thing is, that "influenced" you... Doesn't mean it influenced the other people. Everyone is a unique little program and therefore needs unique inputs in order to produce the desired output. One man's trash is another man's treasure

>>62058962
Nagger

62060621
100$ end of the FUCKING bull run. Here's some influential and intellectual insights

62060648
>>62060540
How do you know. Besides the intent of you making this thread was to educate/shill/ influence the mass retards on this site, don't try to convince me otherwise

62062169
>>62060648
>How do you know.
What do you mean by that? How do I know what?

62062500
>>62060648
How I know that people are unique little programs and that I need to input unique inputs to get the desired outputs? Is that what you're asking or?

62062550
Let me "influence" you young Vishnus of the MINDchain. Listen to one of our spiritual leaders Oliver Talé-Yaźdi how he talks about the Vishnu mindchain. Join our cult *kmh* *kmh* web3 organization where we talk about the new world order.

https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2039256751346700502

62065568
100$ end of MIND RUN

62068446
12 more hours and the thread will be alive for 30 days and that's when Gavin Wood gives me 11.5k DOT for my "influencing" of your young Vishnus of the mindchain.

item.title: It’s all falling apart — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It’s all falling apart

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-04-02 13:44 UTC

OP

At the same time. Bonds dumping, oil spiking, equities crashing

Trump is gonna go down as a worse version of Hoover + LBJ lol

Early replies (first 3)

62068038
>>>/pol/
Trump is not a king
Neither is he responsible for 80 years of BoomerX fuck ups

62068047
>>62068035
it's over

62068065
>>62068038

But he is the closest American president to a king, and the reason we are in this shitshow now. Spiking oil from 60 to 120 in a month is going to rally yields which are going to tighten financial conditions across the country

Tariffs are managed by the legislative, not the executive, but they didn’t stop king Cheeto from wasting a year and dumping markets 20% for being wrong

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62068109
>>62068097
Oil is a [current thing] part of the script. An attempt at 20th century broadcast propaganda methodology to convince the population that technocratic global commutardism isn't that bad at all, just like in the 70s its now time to come together, hold dicks, sing kumbaya and cooperate for continuation of power.

Fuck that

Total war

Anarchy

Kill all BoomerX and the polity

62068129
>>62068106
Yes indeed. Nobody is ever going to provide a positive cashflow in these ponzis ever again

62068148
>>62068077

You’re dumb

>tariffs (power of purse to congress)
>Iran war (power to declare war to congress)
>threatens to sue political opponents
>threatens to sue federal reserve for not lowering rates like we are some 3rd world shithole like turkey
>fights Supreme Court on tariffs and refuses to refund despite them saying he’s wrong
>uses his military police to literally kill American citizens (ICE)

But here’s the facts you’re probably going to dismiss:

>George W. Bush signed 291 Executive orders between 2001 and 2009.

>Barack Obama signed 277 Executive orders between 2009 and 2017.

>Joseph R. Biden, Jr., signed 162 Executive orders between 2021 and 2025.

>Donald J. Trump signed 252 Executive orders between 2025 and 2026.

Read that fucking slowly. A president who cannot pass legislation abuses EO to govern, because he’s an authoritarian

https://www.federalregister.gov/presidential-documents/executive-orders

62068155
>>62068148
You really went all-in on trying to make trump the scapegoat to hide behind to memory hole yourself. A shame that your 20th century broadcast propaganda methodology doesn't work anymore

62068156
>>62068148

Who would have thought? Act like a king, your country becomes like the 3rd world shitholes you look up to

62068165
>>62068156

Now show us the stats on lower court judicial injunctions anon

62068168
>>62068155

Shut the fuck up you dysgenic little 85 IQ mutt. This wasn’t a political thread until you started dickriding. Trump is overseeing the largest tech bubble of all time (Hoover/roaring 20s) and the literal most unpopular war of all time (LBJ Vietnam)

These are comparisons, not statements. I wish people with two digit IQ were gassed

62068177
>>62068038
>>62068075
>>62068089
>>62068109
>62068129
>62068155
clitty leak on aisle 12

62068181
>>62068165
You are arguing against a shill with a script. When the the putting the torch to Rome, which is the entire world, primarily the USA, Canada, the UK and Europe begins this summer, make sure to kill as many BoomerX and left liberals in their temples be it universities, media centers, NGOs... As possible. No mercy

62068191
>>62068155
I remember a time when the average goyim would call the president a nigger regardless of party when they had to pay higher prices for anything, now everything is gay football teams.

62068193
>>62068168

The tech bubble far precedes Trump. It's actually a product of permanent ZIRP policy under Obama after the Bush housing crash.

>the most unpopular war of all time

Is retarded hyperbole, this is hardly a war and it's been a month.

62068197
>>62068177
>>>/s/harty shill
The only good thing your website ever created was Niggercell, Samantha and Arthur

62068203
>>62068191

You really want us to forget what the prior Democrat was doing to us and our country and we're not going to. Funnily enough gas was $4/gallon under him as well. AS WELL AS THE MILLIONS OF NIGGER PEOPLE HE WAS FLOODING IN

I had the FBI in my hometown paying for marketing billboards asking coons to report hate crimes from their fellow whites while we were getting laid off en masse and dumped with murderous foreign hordes

62068217
>>62068191
Your plan failed. Its quite hilarious that after the marketing fuck up with 20th century broadcast propaganda methodology during the coof bail outs, you bet the house again on the same methodology. We are prepared to kill you, yes that includes every single banker and bureaucrat

62068221
>>62068035
Jerome said that private credit is not a systemic risk and I believe him

62068222
>>62068038
I think it's more accurate to say that no one is going to vote for Kamala and trannies and FUCK WHITE MEN because Trump failed.

Leftists and neocons destroying the system only results in white men making a new system without them.

62068236
>>62068222
The time for vooting is over. July 21 the killing begins.

62068245
>>62068193

>Yes, the 2026 war with Iran is considered the most unpopular U.S. conflict at its onset in modern history, exceeding the initial unpopularity of the Vietnam War. Polling consistently shows around 60% of Americans oppose the conflict, marking it as more deeply unpopular at its start than Vietnam or the Iraq War. The Guardian

Hey lil bro, where did I say he caused the AI bubble? Did Hoover cause the roaring 20s? No, but he popped it with retarded policy, like Trump is doing.

62068293
>>62068077
Checked and accurate
FDR the tyrant would like a word lmao
King nigger is unironically pretty high on that list too, just for massively expanding the ndaa and prism
That shit has nothing on tariffs

62068294
>>62068245
Your scripts are hilariously bad p2p advertising shill. You won't memory hole yourself. The bill is due and its paid in your blood in the street. I suggest you carry a cyanide capsule with you at all time

62068302
>>62068245

>literally just started striking Iran a few weeks ago
>YEP IT'S OVER WRAP IT UP MOST UNPOPULAR WAR EVER ABC AND CBS SAY SO

shut the fuck up you stupid coon

62068305
>>62068035
It will all blow over

Right?

62068319
>>62068035
I honestly blame the retarded investors who thought these private credit funds were just like regular old index funds that is very liquid

62068326
>>62068305
No. 70 years of fuck up by left liberals so essentially communists playing ponzi schemes on never to be born children's credit line is not going to just blow over. And as the p2p shills and their equivalent in 20th century media lost the epistemic hegemony its going to be damn bloody

62068344
>>62068305

The Shiller PE ratio was setting new all time highs, right at dotcom bubble levels. The thing retards fail to understand is that the market is a pressure system and it can't go up forever. It was incredibly overpriced and all it needed was a pin prick to correct.

62068374
I mean it just writes itself as this point

>As of April 2026, a 2023 U.S. law requires Congressional approval—a two-thirds Senate majority or an Act of Congress—for any president to withdraw from NATO, restricting unilateral action.

62068387
>>62068374
A senator hanging by the neck cannot voot

62068437
Obama was an effigy; a deliberate homage reflecting the hidden faces behind Western Civilization

These last few years were the psychological primer for this current destabilization initiative; fulfilling the long awaited Phase_FINALIS

America has served its purpose, soon it will sacrificed on the altar and utterly destroyed as prelude for the world that is to come

62068445
>>62068035
>>62068065
post financial positions that match your convictions or gtfo back to your no kangz rally with the rest of the broke women

62068467
>>62068437
>>>/x/
The plan failed shill. When the "adults" in the room want to come as the savior with global commutardism, cbdcs, digital ids and ubi, they get slaughtered

item.title: KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED — 26 replies — 41.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
KABOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** RIPPLE XRP NEWS JUST LEAKED

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-02 13:17 UTC

OP

Ripple is partnering with Convera to expand enterprise cross-border payments.

Together, they’re combining global payment rails with stablecoin-enabled settlement to improve speed, liquidity, and reliability.

>https://x.com/Ripple/status/2038969816187285516 (not fake or clickbait)

Early replies (first 3)

62057459
>>62057451
So bullish

62057468
>>62057451
>Convera

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62057502
Bullish xrp

62057599
uhh can i get a

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057946
>>62057599
checked and BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62057993
>>62057451
>partnering
>enterprise
>cross-border payments
>global payment rails
>stablecoin-enabled settlement
>speed
>liquidity
>reliability

62058568
>>62057993
BOOOOOOOOOOOOZZWORRDSSMM!

62058935
>>62057451
umm embarrassing....?

62058947
BOOM BOOOM BOOM

62061203
BOOOOOOOM

62061366
Keep seething, linkmarines

62061409
Con

62061530
>>62057451
Not buying your heavy bags kike

62062460
>>62058935
>>62057468
These niggers cant google KEEEK

Originally known as Western Union Business Solutions before its 2021 acquisition, Convera reported over USD 190 billion in turnover in 2025 and serves more than 26,000 businesses worldwide. Headquartered in Seattle, it employs 1,001-5,000 people and focuses on tech-driven solutions for FX, compliance, and risk management.

62062479
>>62057451
>leveraging regulated stablecoins for settlement

XRP not needed lol

62062595
>Good news for xrp
>dumps
Never gets old

62062632
>>62062595
It’s literally pumping right now linktard

62062694
>>62058947
I know this is AI, because the backblast would have blown down the door and severely concussed those cats.

62062728
>>62062595
>>Good news for xrp
*good news for Ripple shareholders, not for xrp.

>>62062479

62063129
>>62057451
BOOOOOOOOOOM

62064107
>>62057451
BOOOOOOM

62064147
SO

FXCKING
BULLISH

62066653
>>62057451
BOOOOOOOM

62067672
>>62057451
BOOOOOOOM

62068280
>>62062460
99.99% of people have never heard of em. They also wont be using XRP.

item.title: Eighter oil moons OR we get a recession — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Eighter oil moons OR we get a recession

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-02 13:49 UTC

OP

Why dont schizos get that? There wont be 150 Dollar oil price and a recession. 100 Dollar is the fair price as of now as long as the straight is blocked but no big attack on oilsfields happen and i said that weeks ago.

Early replies (first 3)

62062461
>>62062457
i want bitcoin moon

62062519
>>62062461
And efferiumz

62062551
>>62062457
low iq

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62062555
How red are you panic bro?

62063066
>>62062551
>>62062555
You are like so many people forgetting that a recession immediately lowers oil prices.

62063321
>>62063066
ya but that only happen due reduce demand and increase production in next cycle but this time , iran attacked the supply chain mainly refinery which would take time to go back to normal production ( 6-9 week if war end today)

62063496
>>62063321
But this doesnt affect supply enough to offset the redecued demand in a recession.
Also saudi arabia is fully redirecting its oil with the pipeline. People buy more from russia. People switch to other energies. Higher price already makes people use it less. And so on. That means the current state is fully priced in, a recession would lower the oil price.

62067886
>>62062457
>>62063496
Do u recess an arbitrary amount in a recession? Or only enough to, say destroy 20% of oil demand? Oil needs to go higher than 100 to destroy that much demand, probably. Thx for listening to my TED talk.

62068501
>>62062457
anywhere between 100-150 sounds realistic to me. they already started diverting oil to other harbors.
but it's pointless to discuss it here because 4chan is full of thirdies who just really really need shitcoin numbers for their 500 bucks of disposable income to ever spiral into something significant.
they are the same people who were telling you that silver will go up endlessly because they do not understand supply and demand, and why a higher price also depresses demand.
now watch one of them tell me that the demand for oil is inelastic because he heard it in a video from an oil shill and treats it as gospel now.

62065176

butter my biscuits this guy seems to know something most don't

62065421

I'd have over $50M.

62065945
Green ID and it’s happening

62066038

62066080
Uhh are we the new stinkies?

62066141
>>62065421
I'd have $200M.

62066682
>>62063246
This week you say?

So bullish

62067700
>>62063246
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM **MASSIVE** EXPLOSION

62068313
>>62063246

item.title: BREAKING NEWS: THE RIPPLE CEO HAS JUST LEAKED THE SECRET SAUCE FOR XRP — 10 replies — 24.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
BREAKING NEWS: THE RIPPLE CEO HAS JUST LEAKED THE SECRET SAUCE FOR XRP

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-02 13:30 UTC

OP

Something massive is brewing

Early replies (first 3)

62066793
>>62066670
let me guess, is the secret sauce a nigger hiding in the xrp blockchain?

62066898
>>62066670
Bullish for chainlink

62066945
Hmmmmm. Set my money on fire. Or ripples……hmmmm set my money on fire?….or buy the ripple….hmmm mmm,

HMMMMMM MM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62066950
>>62066945
Thats a clear bottom

62067091
>>62066670
Dafug does this nigga mean with 13T facilitated last year?

They bought GTreasury which did that, and now renamed it.
Its like buying a mcdonalds, renaming it burgerking and now bragging about how burgerking did 15 million in sales last year lmao

62067102
>>62066670
The harder they push it the more it won't happen.

62067148
>>62066670
Ripple's "secret sauce" is a software platform that isn't onchain and doesn't need xrp.

Ripple really knows how to keep the L in XRPL.

62067709
>>62067091
Xrp are the biggest lyers in crypto.

62068258
>>62067091
>They bought GTreasury which did that, and now renamed it.
The xrp retards eat it up too. I really hope this faggot goes to jail or dies at the hands of an angry xrp holder.

62068380
>>62066945
Zoom out you disingenuous cocksucker

item.title: >Legitimate quantum threat to BTC from Google >Bitcoin price doesn't move at all That's how you know it's bad. — 37 replies — 23.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Legitimate quantum threat to BTC from Google >Bitcoin price doesn't move at all That's how you know it's bad.

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-04-02 12:22 UTC

OP

>Legitimate quantum threat to BTC from Google
>Bitcoin price doesn't move at all
That's how you know it's bad. It's so bad that coiners are absolutely rejecting the reality of their situation. Literally rocking back and forth and saying everything is fine.

You can see it all over X:
>Well if this was true then everything would be destroyed, not just Bitcoin
>If they hack Bitcoin, it would make it worthless, so therefore nobody would ever hack it
>We'll just fix the protocol and migrate to it, ezpz

Absolute fucking copium lmao. So now they're just pretending everything is fine.

Early replies (first 3)

62064120
>>62064115
shut up!!!

62064602
>>62064115

Quantum computers don't exist.

62064636
>I can factor 21 therefore your coin is worthless

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62064741
>>62064723
I'm shorting it because of the right reasons and will go long for the right reasons when the price reaches where I expect a real bounce.
It has nothing to do with quantum clickbait that comes out. Only fools like you think something real is going on there.

62064776
>>62064723
Yeah, you're definitely the smartest big boy crypto investor on the planet
(Or, you're wrong)

62064777
>>62064741
>all bitcoin "investors" behave just like me
you must be new and never seen the absolute waves of retard bitcoin investors overreacting to clickbait news that is 1/100th as consequential as what google announced. the odds of everyone suddenly becoming a cool and collected sophisticated investor and stoicly not reacting is 0.

no, the waves of cryptobro retards are denying reality is what's happening. they haven't suddenly become dispassionate hedge fund managers. they're plugging their ears and hoping the existential threat goes away.
>but I'm not doing that!
good for you, you're marginally less retarded than your peers

62064786
>>62064777
you're a complete dumbass, not worth interacting with. it must be hard to be you or with you.

62064794
>>62064777

> getting spooked over threats that don't exist that have solutions, if the materialize

checked

62064815
>>62064115
>Bottom signal FUD deployed

62064831
>>62064786
>you're not worth interacting with!!!
>(You)
extremely feminine behavior

>>62064794
>i didn't exist during covid and ftx, when retards panic crashed the market
lmao sweet summer child

62064832
>>62064115
I find her very attractive.

62064848
>>62064815
>trading on an opinion on /biz/
bitcoin traders continue to prove their "sophistication"
by all means, let me influence you to buy more

62064858
>>62064831

Did Bitcoin go to zero or did it hit another all time high, almost doubling the previous ath?

62064868
>>62064858
Retards panicked at non-existential-threat news and crashed, while retards are currently not panicking at existential-threat news. But you know that, and you're being willfully dense and pretending not to understand, it's ok.

62064882
>>62064868
>not panicking at existential-threat news
because they know it isn't a threat

62064898
>>62064868

You're the one pretending that everyone is a trader with low time preference who doesn't understand what they're invested in and trying to change the subject.

Everyone isn't a moron who sells their assets every time someone comes up with new fud.

62064913
what a retarded post and retarded strawman.
Bitcoin will adopt sphincs+ based schemes. These are the most conservative post-quantum schemes we have. They are already implemented on the liquid network as shrincs signatures.
The security assumptions of bitcoin will go from sha256 + ecdsa to only sha256.
Meanwhile the shitcoiners (the co-author of the paper works for the ethereum foundation) will need to adopt complex untested schemes to keep their pos consensus layer working.
So yeah, bitcoin has a straightfoward path unlike pos chains. You want these larger signatures to be adopted now when the strongest QC don't even have 100 logical qubits? you're retarded.

62064917
>>62064882
>they know it's not an existential threat
Yeah, that's why they're saying
>If they hack Bitcoin, it would make it worthless, so therefore nobody would ever hack it

Imagine your defense being
>The market can panic faster than the attacker can liquidate

62064924
>>62064917

Do you use google chrome, anon?

62064929
>>62064917
>11 likes
sincerely why are you combing the xeets of literal whos?

62064943
>>62064929
>not enough likes
don't pretend that if i spent 5 more minutes to find one with x10 likes, it would convince you, you weasel

62065107
>>62064913
POS stands for piece of shit for those wondering

62065205
I looked into this a year ago. BTC devs were already hundreds of messages deep into debating which encryption upgrade to use. Many of these were already being coded. It’ll be fine unless someone drops a zero-day quantum computer ten years ahead of schedule.

62065298
>>62064929
why are you in every thread policing xitter likes? twerp

62065340
>google makes the first quantum computer
>they're the only ones who have it
>the only quantum computer on Earth
>they randomly decide to use it to attack Bitcoin of all fucking things
quantum FUD is for retarded brainlets only

62065485
>>62064777
...
Are you black?

62066130
>>62064115
Worst case scenario is a hard fork to quantum proof algo.
Most likely scenario is that quantum stays a pipe dream that keeps being "two decades away" forever like thorium reactors.

62066268
>>62066130
You can't migrate Satoshi's P2PK transactions. Any solution to this causes a huge rift in the community. You either hard code a blacklist of a bunch of old wallets that you HOPE are all Satoshi's, but this establishes a bad precedent, or you let Satoshi's funds be stolen and dilute the fuck out of the supply, crashing the price.

62066736
>>62066268
Just slurp the Satoshi sats when that happens
Easy peasy

62066768
>>62064741
>quantum clickbait
What are you saying, the tech isn’t real?

62066964
Ok build a quantum computer with 1200 qubits then if it's so easy

62067928
>>62064115
Here's BTC's problem. They wanted offchain prespent transactions so badly (lightning network/segwit) that they based their entire network model on the requirement that every user be able to run a raspberry pi node to verify transactions. How will raspberry pi's keep up with the overhead for quantum-resistant hashing?

62067936
>LOAD ZE QUANTUM FUD

item.title: Sorry incels, but women are better investors — 41 replies — 55.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sorry incels, but women are better investors

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-04-02 13:10 UTC

OP

New research confirms a superior performance by female traders

Women have had a pretty bad reputation in global financial markets. As investors, the perception is that they joined the game too late, they saved too little and they invested too conservatively.

But a growing number of surveys and research papers suggest this is a stereotype that is out of date. The reality is that women actually spend more time researching their options than men, are better at matching their investments to their life goals, tend to trade less and when they do, remain calmer during the storms that unnerve male investors on the financial high seas.

So, recent publicity around an NAB report suggesting that women are losing large sums of money because they are too conservative in their investment choices, has raised hackles. Not least because it claims there are anatomical differences in the brains of men and women that influence investment decisions.

One of the most in-depth pieces of research to support the view that women make better investors has come from UNSW Business School

The road the academics travelled was a long one, encompassing a 17-year period, and focused on Finland due to the transparency of its financial marketplace.

The study matched daily trade by male and female individual investors in Nokia and 27 other major Finnish stocks and found that there was a continuous female compounded rate of return of 21.44% per annum, rising to 43.16% per annum just for the main stock, Nokia.

Women individual investors also outperformed domestic and international institutional investors with an even more substantial gain of €1,654.24 million.

Early replies (first 3)

62064583
Women are good bear indicators

62064621
Did you seriously write that crap as bait? Stopped reading after Sorry incels

62064660
>>62064621
OP did a good job
>>62064576
You did a good job. I read ALL that. I notice women tend to be a lot of important economic research and professors. Perhaps their holistic way of think is advantaged.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62065219
>>62064576
Western women aren't good at anything except taking black dick and complaining for gibs.

This article is complete nonsense.

62065308
I'm calling bullshit. 99.9% of women do not have the temperament for trading.

62065313
>>62065308
Investing, not trading

62065314
Are women aware that I don't give a fuck what they do with their 401k.
>17 year period
So I guess from 2000-2017 women invested in blue chips or some shit while men gambled on Swiggity Niggity corp and other tech and housing shit, but since women were less likely to blow up their accounts trying to GET BIG OR DIE they're 'better investors'.

Well good for them, but it's not relevant to me.

62065320
>>62065075
If I had taken risks in 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 etc. on risky shots like Nvidia instead of listenign to 'broad market thesis' I would have 100xed. Instead I get to listen to idiots tell me simultaneously that women are better investrs because no risks and 'hurr durr but you didn't put all your money in nvidia calls did you genius?" because people are unable to admit that taking a calculated but crazy risk is usually the correct answer unless you want to be Wagebert for the rest of your life.

62065328
>>62065313
>investing
lmfao, that means in the current environment of an inverted demographic pyramid being a greater fool that holds bags that have no bid and are contractually going to have to be sold by pension funds to cover the contractual pay outs to retirees that outnumber active population 2:1.

Lets make a quick calculation:
When 2 Boomers need to have 3000 dollars in shares sold to cover their pay outs and 0 dollar enter the ponzi, how long does it take for the foids 401k to go to 0

62065347
>>62065075
Exactly. Thats why they tell people to just DCA into ETFs. No male or female traders needed.

62065411
>>62065328
tl;dr I lost all my money in memecoins while stacy gets rich

62065440
>>62064576
>finland

62065468
>>62065411
stacy isnt rich. Stacy was stupid enough to pay all her subsidized cash flow into a 401k she has no access too while the pension fund that manages the account has obligations to provide a monthly pay out to Boomer Joes that outnumber stacys contribution not just 2:1 but more like 25:1

62065472
>>62065320
>buying nvidia
>taking risk
nigger you would have bought some fly by night penny stock because it was shilled on wall street bets and lost all your money. Anyone with money in the s&p had exposure to nvidia and outperformed your sorry ass

62065552
>>62065468
>not like me that YOLO'd that money into memecoins

62065577
>>62065552
Are you projecting? My currently preferred method of cashflow is a bit more hand on, beside betting on stacy and her trust in the ponzi schemes of the 20th century going belly up. I will likely keep on doing both, robbing people is actually quite fun.

62065817
Why are moids so bad at everything, sisters?

62065837
>>62065817
>t.50 year old grey haired freak larping as woman

62066208
Lol at the malding moid itt

62066337
>>62064576
looks like another fine study by Yas Kween University

62067044
It's crazy to observe how it all stems from biology. Historically, most women got to have kids, while men have to do risky shit to try to be in the top. Starting from dating/reproduction, they don't have to take any risk. Most activities they partake in involves no risk, just various amounts of work. This is why corporations are full of women and startup companies have close to zero. They don't want to waste their 20s to chasing a potential lottery ticket. They can just stay fit and wear makeup and wait at the finish line to be with the one who won the lottery ticket.
>women are better investors
yeah because if you generalize, they just buy whatever is popular, I guess. Literally their "castle in the sky" theory thinking is built in. If one woman is attracted to invest in something, chances are every other woman has the same feeling, therefore that particular stock's volume will increase. The women I know that invest basically just buy stocks they hear about from their friends/news. Or simply just let their boyfriends do the investing.

What annoys me is that, as humans, we need to take risky decisions, we cannot wait for the full information, but since women won't take any, men are left to take a disproportionately higher risk to carry both genders. This is so evident in many aspects of life that I can't even remember most of them right now. But it checks out and I think about that whole "men have the burden meaning" thing. It should be a human thing, but women won't do any of that, so its up to men to do it all. Like being the guy that does the whole project work in college and others just taking credit.

62067069
>>62067044
>women live longer
yeah because they never need to take any risk, they can just focus on their own health and wellbeing 100% of the time. Government, corporations, society, boyfriend will just take care of everything else for them. The first time they do take risk and it doesn't work out it fucks them up so much they don't know what hit them. Then they become even more risk averse.
What pisses me off is the inequality of it, how men just have to carry the risk for both genders so that women can just do nothing, essentially. Then they just judge men for being stupid and uneducated.
>muh college degrees!
The whole education system is built around women and their temperament. Pair this with the fact that they don't need to take any risk, its easy to see how they can just perform well in courses made for them, in a world that coddles them, with group projects where they can just pick and choose what they work on etc. They get a false sense of superiority, not their privilege, then act all high and mighty "oh I can do it as a girl, why can't men?". You ever notice how they plan their lives a decade in advance?
>at age X I will get married
>at age Y I will have kids
They never need to take risks or have any doubts about the future. They are just living life on a treadmill.

62067092
>>62064576
outperforming a majority of traders is not hard, just buy and hold index funds

62067107
>>62067069
cont.
I don't even think its a grand conspiracy or "patriarchy". It's raw evolutionary wiring clashing with a modern environment that has removed most natural checks on female risk-aversion while amplifying male disposability.
It's all biology:
>females are the scare reproductive resource
>males are abundant, high-variance side
success for men for men was always a high-stakes tournament, be it hunting, innovating, fighting. Failure meant you didn't reproduce.

We've built a world that insulates women from the downsides of their preferences while still demanding men absorb the variance.

also women making fun of men when they are sick: WE LITERALLY HAVE STRONGER IMMUNE SYSTEMS THAT HIT US LIKE A TRUCK WHEN WE HAVE THE FLU, THATS WHY.

Don't even get me started on the cultural gaslighting known as female empowerment while quietly relying on male disposability to handle the uncertainty, innovation, defense, and dirty work.

62067374
>>62067044
>>62067107
based birbs

62067408
>>62065472
it was a risk back then because people kept saying it was the top and it couldn't go higher.

62067435
>>62064576
The game is over, as I am fond of saying.

The women won. They are in control of the eugenics program and the multi-trillion-dollar pussy reward system — a highly symbolic fertility ritual. But they are in control of much more than the reproductive apparatus. They control not only the men and government of the United States of America but through the power of pussy and the susceptibility of men and government officials, fiat currency manipulation by the Witches of Fiat Currency at the Federal Reserve System, a large army of simps and "backers" (this is a technically incorrect use of the term) who are fighting to maintain control of the Frankenstein monster they created called Onlyfans (in a war I fear we are going to lose), and even the Republican form of government they created which inevitably—and I would argue by design—fosters centralized female matrilineal control and assures only a handful of "pre-selected" men must be sacralized to control an entire country, they control the entire world.

62067469
>>62064576
Who cares.

62067471
>>62067435
>that image

62067477
>>62064576
the riskiest trade is the risk free one
the cheapest price will cost you the dearest

62067535
>remain calmer during the storms that unnerve male investors on the financial high seas.

LMAO this is not true my mom is on suicide watch every time S and P dips

62067721
>>62065075
>women only follow the broad market
Kekked and also internally consistent

62068207
>>62067044
>>62067069
>>62067107
No one forced you to take risks, moid
No one forced you to drop out of college
No one forced you to throw your money into shitcoins
You could just have gone for the cushy degree and job, and put your money into etfs

item.title: What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday. — 83 replies — 21.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck is going on with MSFT? How is this not a great entry? Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-04-02 10:05 UTC

OP

What the fuck is going on with MSFT?
How is this not a great entry?
Convince me to not go all in by monday.

Early replies (first 3)

62043952
because the music has stopped

fucking cretin

62043962
Just buy gold and gold mining stocks.

62043971
>>62043947
I would wait until i see signs of of absolute panic.
And even then only go in for a medium term trade.
Liquidity is not great and there are big IPOs coming that will absorb even more of it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62051391
>>62043947
Because when you go all in on AI, you lose. Shrimple as.

62051399
Great entry for a short

62051403
>>62050633
No one went broke betting on the turkey before thanksgiving

62051497
>>62043947
the company is jeeted to a point where you have to wonder if they can ever get back from that

62051499
Biz as always, low IQ people blaming everything on indians. Like if MICROSOFT hasn't been shit forever. But now, it's all indians fault. Sure buddies. Fucking retards

62051760
>>62049635
>>62049655
You're burying the lead. The level of goodwill they lost from the Xbone cannot be understated. They were saying insane shit like the kinect would be able to count how many people were in your living room watching a movie, and the rental price would dynamically change to accommodate it. And you wouldnt even be able to lend your game discs out, theyd be hard linked to your console.

Only for Sony to hit the stage at the next E3 and announce a "dynamic" new way to lend your game to friends. Only for one exec to walk up, then cheekily hand his ps4 disc to another exec. And some shit about all processing being handled in "the cloud", which gamers immediately saw through as Oh we wont really own our games theyll be on Microshit's servers forever. It was a fucking debacle all around. Even the price, Xbone was $499, PS4 was $399.

62051770
”we support bipoc trans gamers” - minecraft title screen after jeetsoft acquisition

62051787
>>62043947
In those 3 peaks I see
>exuberance
>metered enthusiasm that is beginning to wane
>desperation
where we are now, it might try to rechallenge the previous high, but it will fall short and then we'll start to see the 'exposé' articles hit the news after it starts plummeting
I have no position in ms, just how I see it going down

62051789
>>62051499

>Microsoft was always a mixed bag
>now there are Indians on top of that

how does this not make sense to you

62051794
The company is failing because it is too jeeted. The cheap labor pumped up the stock to astronomically high levels. But now the effects of that are lowering the value of the company product. So the sock has to go down as investors look to alternatives for better products.

62051822
>>62051497
Usually when the rot (Indians or other nepo groups) gets past a certain points its just irreversibly fucked.
Not a collaspe by any means but just slowly fading into irrelevance and incompetence like the legacy tech companies (IBM, HPE, whatever)

62051849
>>62051822

IBM is the best example. It was huge. America's first premier tech company. It should still be so. But nope. They were the first to take the mass Indianification poison pill, greedy execs looking for cheaper workers, until it backfired and the Indian clan had enough collective power to take it over entirely. Now it's just an eternally irrelevant dinosaur and they squat on the embers collecting whatever spare pennies they can off of it.

62051889
>>62051499
msft was garbage first, but they improved and XP and later Win7 were kinda ok, but then sudenly they started implementing jeets and everything went to shit

62051895
>>62043947
MacroHard gonna pummel Microjeet like a Pajeeta girl on her first night off the plane in Texas getting pounded by big white cock.

62052017
>>62049474
and it still isn’t worth it to use

62052128
>>62043947

It’s because they attached themselves to the hip to openAI. Same as oracle, market knows Altman is a scammer

62053062
>>62048780
Azure is also trash, even open AI is trying to switch to AWS and they opened a law suit against them.

62053066
>>62049594
>They deserve to die for what they did to Halo
This.

62054681
>>62050175
sybau

62054695
>>62043947
No Indian company should be worth 2.67T

62054705
>>62047799
Remember that firmware update from Microsoft a few months back that supposedly was a bug that fried few peoples SSDs?
Good times

62054994
>>62051399
no it isnt, you shouldve shorted months ago lol now its a long ish wait 2-3 weeks for hammer fists

62056532
>>62054695
True.
And everyone hates America now.
Many are moving away from Microjeet and Azure.

62061658
>>62047766
DIS NIGGA USIN ICT LMAO
DOW Theory for zoomers

62061676
>>62043947
>since this post 4 days ago, it has began a head-n-shoulders off support at 350
Good luck believers.

62063135
>>62049970
there's an indicator that does that automatically

62066090
I am on Win11, and I recently renamed my headphones through the Settings app. That's it, just renamed them. Windows for some reason disabled them entirely, and I had to manually reenable them by digging through 5 menus to find the right setting. It's beyond garbage, I'm only using Windows because of gaming, but Steam runs on Linux too, so even there MS is losing its edge.

62066117
This is the century of Linux

62066120
>>62043947
they went all in on AI and AI price is gonna skyrocket, they're fucked

62067431
>>62061658
Ict isn’t based on Dow theory iirc retard

item.title: Another Saylor just dropped. Now he's posting AI generated videos of females telling you to buy STRC and get 11% yield.… — 43 replies — 13.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another Saylor just dropped. Now he's posting AI generated videos of females telling you to buy STRC and get 11% yield.…

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-04-02 10:48 UTC

OP

Another Saylor just dropped. Now he's posting AI generated videos of females telling you to buy STRC and get 11% yield. He is desperate

Early replies (first 3)

62053644
>>62053587

62053647
fuck shkreli more than anything

saylor will be the first trillionaire

62053648
>>62053587
Or usurious bank yields are really that predatory these days in comparison

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62054251
>>62054141
>a usa jew said a balkan jew was cool and a goy believed it

62054306
>>62054102
L'chayim good evening!

62054825
>>62054185
This, Shkreli is pretty with pharma, but retarded when it comes to tech

62054830
>>62053647
Shkreli is based, I watch all his livestreams

62054909
>>62054141
https://youtu.be/80BXR4zX2EE?si=OTMdmOm9Z3kWmPPM
16 minute video.

62055163
>>62054909
dude just GIVE ME the RUNDOWN!!!

62055248
>>62054306
My nigga shkreli isn't a jew

62055660
>>62053587
If they were real girls and occasionally said they loved and were proud of me in between telling me to buy $STRC, this would work on me.
t. khhv who spends all his income on OF, ASMR, and Twitch streamers and would die for Israel or whatever if they got a qt to tell me she needed me to. Luckily they haven't figured out how extreme a simp I am.

62055692
>>62053587
>another crypto rug pull
>11% yield
No thanks I can bleed money, have tech exposure and still get 11% with JEPQ

62055828
>>62053587
How long till the SEC gets involved with this scammer?

62055962
>>62055828
At the very least it would have to wait until the republicans are out of office so the SEC can be refunded and directed to do their job again

62056339
We literally have a scammer using AI females to catch simps to give him money to leverage buy bitcoin

This is not gonna end well

62057831
>>62055660
Bro do nofap or something, this is no way to exist

62059850
>>62057831
>>62055660
Or just get a practice gf or fwb (its easier than you think) to get that energy out. You get more positive energy rebound that way.

62059950
>>62053647
I don't agree with his drug price gouging despite his copes, but I don't really care because he gives out a lot of interesting information. His sycophants are degenerate though lol.

62059975
>>62053587
anything shkreli says is bad, is good.

62060091
>>62054079
How?

62060099
>twitter screenshot thread

62061010
>>62059850
>practice gf
I consider this deeply immoral.
>fwb
I'm not sure I would be able to resist falling in love with her.
>>62057831
>nofap
It wouldn't help much I don't think. It might even make it worse for a bit.

62061053
This dipshit makes me want to sell my BTC so bad. Can't believe how retarded he is.

62061149
>>62053587
A criminal calling out another criminal? What is this, criminalception?

62061255
>>62053660
Kicked dog syndrome. Dumb goys have been abused so long they dont even know what decent treatment looks like. Its perfectly normal to give someone 100% of your money, let them lend it out for free, then hoard 94-99% of the profit for themselves.

62061323
>>62053587
that's like d grade rates

62061414
Shabbat shalolm

62061447
Rents due.

62062037
>>62061010
Practice gf is a soft euphemism, but yes I agree doing that on purpose would be immoral
>couldn't resist falling in live w fwb
That is a legitimate trap, and good on you for being honest w yourself about it. It takes a certain amount of heartbreak and cynicism to really get there. The point of the comment was that you need to just get a bad relationship out of the way so you have a far better idea of what you want.
>nofap would be worse
It is worse for approximately 10 days. A lot worse. You'll relapse many times before you hit that mark. You still should try it anyway as an exercise in discipline. This sounds difficult to believe with your current mindset, but after 3 to 4 weeks you don't even think about masturbation anymore except as a novelty

62062196
>>62053587
I don't want to like Saylor but everytime I see a jew kvetching because of him it makes me want to buy his stock.

62066982
>>62062196
This, I'm not touching Saylor's products but when BTC rips to 1m+ he will have like 1m Bitcoin and be worth over 1 trillion dollars.
He's an autistic adult gaming the entire financial system and he's probably going to win.

You know the jews want to liquidate him, but he's probably positioned just slightly better than coinbase.

62066996
MARTIN SHKRELI REDEMPTION ARC

62067553
to be fair STRC actually looks pretty good. surprised its still hodling up.
